name: thread_not_removed
code:
proof fn phi_1_thread_not_removed(old_self: Os, new_self: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.thread_dom() == old_self.thread_dom().remove(thread_ptr),
    ensures
        new_self.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
{
}
reason: The killed thread should be removed from the domain; if it persists, the kill had no effect on thread membership.

name: thread_dom_empty_after_kill
code:
proof fn phi_2_thread_dom_empty_after_kill(old_self: Os, new_self: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.thread_dom() == old_self.thread_dom().remove(thread_ptr),
    ensures
        new_self.thread_dom() =~= Set::<ThreadPtr>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Killing a single thread should not empty the entire thread domain; if it does, the spec over-constrains removal.

name: other_thread_also_removed
code:
proof fn phi_3_other_thread_also_removed(old_self: Os, new_self: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr, other_ptr: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        old_self.thread_dom().contains(other_ptr),
        thread_ptr != other_ptr,
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.thread_dom() == old_self.thread_dom().remove(thread_ptr),
        threads_unchanged_except(old_self.proc_man, new_self.proc_man, set![]),
    ensures
        !new_self.thread_dom().contains(other_ptr),
{
}
reason: Killing one thread must not silently remove a different thread from the domain; the spec should preserve all other threads.

name: owned_threads_always_empty
code:
proof fn phi_4_owned_threads_always_empty(old_self: Os, new_self: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.get_proc(old_self.get_thread(thread_ptr).owning_proc).owned_threads@ ==
            old_self.get_proc(old_self.get_thread(thread_ptr).owning_proc).owned_threads@.remove_value(thread_ptr),
        new_self.get_proc(old_self.get_thread(thread_ptr).owning_proc).owned_threads.len() ==
            old_self.get_proc(old_self.get_thread(thread_ptr).owning_proc).owned_threads.len() - 1,
    ensures
        new_self.get_proc(old_self.get_thread(thread_ptr).owning_proc).owned_threads@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Killing one thread should only decrement the owning process's thread count by 1, not force it to zero regardless of how many threads the process owns.

name: threads_unchanged_empty_set_contradiction
code:
proof fn phi_5_threads_unchanged_empty_set_contradiction(old_self: Os, new_self: Os, thread_ptr: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self.thread_dom().contains(thread_ptr),
        new_self.wf(),
        new_self.thread_dom() == old_self.thread_dom().remove(thread_ptr),
        threads_unchanged_except(old_self.proc_man, new_self.proc_man, set![]),
        new_self.proc_dom() == old_self.proc_dom(),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: threads_unchanged_except with the empty set claims NO threads are excepted from the unchanged invariant, yet a thread was removed; if this derives false the spec postconditions are mutually contradictory.
